text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously constructive. Tech led H1 gains and Nomura is backing the chip rally on hyperscaler spending — that's direct fuel for your book. But ADP came in soft (98K vs expectations), which keeps rate-cut hopes alive but also signals the economy is softening. Soft macro + chip-specific tailwind = choppy grind higher, not a clean rip.

Your holdings: All HOLD/NEW, nothing screaming danger. SOXL (+4.8%) is a 3x leveraged chip ETF — it decays and swings hard; on margin that's a double whammy. Know your $200 stop and don't let a bad day turn into a disaster. SPAL is brand-new, very volatile, and moves fast — tiny position if you're on margin. QQQI (+0.67%) is lagging everything; fine as income, but don't let it crowd out better ideas.

Watch: Friday's jobs report (real payrolls, not ADP) could reprice rate expectations fast. Also watch NVDA for any reaction to the Nomura chip note.

Bottom line: Your chip/tech core is in good shape — protect the leveraged names and don't chase new buys into a premium market on soft macro.
plays: SOXL: Know your $200 hard stop. On margin + 3x leverage, a bad macro print can slice through gains in one session — size accordingly.
SPAL: Brand-new leveraged ETF, moves fast. Keep position small; respect the $21.38 stop without exception given margin exposure.
No new buys today — scanner setups exist but most are in daily premium (chasing), and soft ADP data doesn't give a clean macro green light right now.
